notes: Three real cases, one per JB Agentic Meetup #1 talk. Each input is the talk's genuine auto-transcript (<slug>.en.raw.srt) plus its slide deck, staged into stage/<slug>/ by prepare.py (run it first; stage/ is gitignored). Graded on invariants (valid SRT, no empties, contiguous, timestamps subset of input, UTF-8 no BOM) + filler-density drop + per-talk term spot-checks against the slide-canonical forms, since each speaker's own polish legitimately differs from the skill's. The real transcripts mangle terms differently: Khew's 'IaC'/'Azure', Kowa's 'cloud'->'Claude'/'Jason'->'JSON', while Soh's terms are mostly transcribed correctly so its value is invariants + filler cleanup + the OpenClaw restraint.

evals.name: real-khewjiapeng
evals.prompt: Can you polish the subtitles for my talk? The raw transcript and the slide deck are in agentic/evals/subtitle-polish/stage/JBAgentic-20260530-meetup-1-KhewJiaPeng-AIMeetsInfrastructure/ — the .en.raw.srt is the auto-generated SRT, and slides/ plus slides.pdf are the deck. Clean it up and save the polished version next to it.
evals.expected_output: A polished SRT (raw marker dropped from the stem) that strips fillers/stutters and fixes mis-transcribed terms against the slides, holding the invariants: no empty cues, contiguous numbering (cues may be dropped+renumbered but no holes), surviving cues keep their original timestamps, UTF-8 without BOM. The speaker mangles 'IaC' many ways (IAC, ISC, ISA, ICNA, 'I a C') and 'Azure' ('Asia bottle'); these should converge to the slide-canonical forms.
assertions: Output SRT exists with the .raw marker removed from the stem
Output parses as valid SRT (index, timing, body per cue)
No empty cues in the output
Cue numbering is contiguous from 1 with no holes
Every output cue's timestamp is present in the input (no timestamps invented or shifted)
Output is UTF-8 without a BOM
Filler density (standalone um/uh) is lower in the output than in the raw input
The term 'IaC' appears in slide-canonical form and the mis-hearings (IAC/ISC/ISA/ICNA/'I a C') are gone
'Azure portal' is recovered from 'Asia bottle' mis-transcriptions where context supports it
Affirmations and Singlish particles (lah/ya) are preserved, not scrubbed

evals.name: real-kowajialiang
evals.prompt: I have a rough auto-transcript of a meetup talk that needs cleaning up — fillers and some wrong technical terms. The raw transcript and the talk's slides are in agentic/evals/subtitle-polish/stage/JBAgentic-20260530-meetup-1-KowaJiaLiang-OurAIJourney/ (the .en.raw.srt and the slides/ folder beside it). Please polish it and save the result alongside.
evals.expected_output: A polished SRT (raw marker dropped) holding the invariants. The transcriber heard the product 'Claude' as 'cloud' throughout (including 'cloud code' -> 'Claude Code' and 'cloud MD' -> 'CLAUDE.md') and 'JSON' as 'Jason'; these should converge to slide-canonical forms while a genuinely generic 'cloud' may remain. 'MCP'/'API' are already correct and must survive. Singlish particles (ya/okay/lah) preserved.
assertions: Output SRT exists with the .raw marker removed from the stem
Output parses as valid SRT (index, timing, body per cue)
No empty cues in the output
Cue numbering is contiguous from 1 with no holes
Every output cue's timestamp is present in the input (no timestamps invented or shifted)
Output is UTF-8 without a BOM
Filler density (standalone um/uh) is lower in the output than in the raw input
'cloud' is corrected to 'Claude' where it means the product (a few stray 'cloud' allowed)
'Claude Code' appears, recovered from 'cloud code'
'Jason' is corrected to 'JSON'
'MCP' is preserved (already correct in the raw)
Singlish particles and affirmations (ya/okay/lah) are preserved, not scrubbed

evals.name: real-sohjunwei
evals.prompt: This SRT is an auto-transcribed meetup talk that needs filler cleanup and a consistency pass. The raw transcript and the deck are in agentic/evals/subtitle-polish/stage/JBAgentic-20260530-meetup-1-SohJunWei-AIForEverydayFriction/ (the .en.raw.srt and the slides/ folder beside it). Clean it up for me and write the polished file next to the raw one.
evals.expected_output: A polished SRT (raw marker dropped) holding the invariants. This transcript got the product names mostly right (Hermes, Telegram, MCP, dashboard, database), so the skill must preserve them rather than 'correct' them. Restraint: 'OpenClaw' is the speaker's own word (the slides say 'OpenRouter') and must be left as-is. Fillers/stutters removed; Singlish particles preserved.
assertions: Output SRT exists with the .raw marker removed from the stem
Output parses as valid SRT (index, timing, body per cue)
No empty cues in the output
Cue numbering is contiguous from 1 with no holes
Every output cue's timestamp is present in the input (no timestamps invented or shifted)
Output is UTF-8 without a BOM
Filler density (standalone um/uh) is lower in the output than in the raw input
'Hermes' is preserved (transcribed correctly in the raw, not mangled)
'Telegram' is preserved
'MCP' is preserved
Restraint: 'OpenClaw' is preserved and NOT rewritten to the slide term 'OpenRouter'
Singlish particles and affirmations (ya/lah/ah) are preserved, not scrubbed
